title: Private to Public Company Conversion Online in India – Process & Cost
metaDescription: What is private to public conversion? Convert private company to public limited online in India. Check process, cost, documents required & expert service.

hero.headline: Private to Public Company Conversion in India – Online Process & Cost
hero.subtext: Unlock capital markets and scale your business. Seamless ROC compliance by Professionals Law.

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: The conversion of a Private Limited Company into a Public Limited Company is a strategic move that enables greater access to capital, wider investor participation and enhanced credibility. Under the Companies Act, 2013, the same involves the amendment of the AOA and the passing of resolutions of board as well as shareholders and obtaining the approval from the ROC. A Public Limited Company may invite the public for subscription of its shares and can also sell its securities in the open market. Professionals Law is into the business of taking care of end-to-end conversion right from legal drafting to ROC, all adhering to the statutory requirements and taking you through the strategic manoeuvre to expand your corporate charts with ease.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: The conversion from Private to Public Limited Company is commonly required when a business wants to publicly raise the capital by issuing its shares and listing them on stock exchange and also to make a prominent public image. This conversion will help gain greater financial agility, corporate transparency and business growth opportunities. With Professionals Law, this process is executed in full regulatory compliance, meeting all statutory requirements swiftly and bringing your company into a position to invite public scrutiny, investment, listing, etc., if desired.

body.title: Access to Public Capital
body.description: A Public Limited Company can raise funds from the general public through IPOs and issue shares and debentures freely, expanding financial growth opportunities.

body.title: Enhanced Market Reputation
body.description: Public companies enjoy higher credibility and trust among stakeholders, customers and financial institutions.

body.title: Improved Liquidity of Shares
body.description: Shareholders can freely transfer their shares, provide better liquidity and exit options.

body.title: Eligibility for Listing
body.description: A Public Limited Company can get listed on stock exchanges, increasing market valuation and visibility.

body.title: Growth and Expansion Potential
body.description: With access to a broader investor base, public companies can scale operations and enter new markets more easily.

criteria: The company must have a minimum of 7 shareholders and 3 directors.
No restrictions on the transfer of shares should exist in the AOA.
The name should end with “Limited” instead of “Private Limited”.
Necessary board and shareholder approvals must be obtained.

documents: KYC documents of all the directors and shareholders
Altered Memorandum and Articles of Association
Declaration by directors and managing director
Copy of the latest audited financial statements
Certificate of Incorporation and PAN of the company

body.description: Call a board meeting to propose conversion and approve the notice of an EGM.
Pass special resolutions for the conversion, name change and alteration of AOA and MOA.
Submit the special resolutions with the Registrar within 30 days.
File the application for conversion of the company with necessary attachments.
ROC reviews the application and issues a new Certificate of Incorporation upon approval.
Update all statutory registers, bank details and government departments with the new status.
Failure to file necessary forms (MGT-14 or INC-27) may result in penalties under Section 117 of the Companies Act.
Any misrepresentation or non-compliance can attract fines up to ₹1 lakh and further fines per day of default.
Delay in updating statutory records may result in legal or financial repercussions.

body.question: Is government approval required for converting a private company into a public company?
body.answer: No special government approval is required, but ROC approval is mandatory.

body.question: What is the minimum capital required for a Public Limited Company?
body.answer: The minimum authorized capital must be ₹5 lakhs.

body.question: Can a newly incorporated private company convert into a public company?
body.answer: Yes, provided it meets all eligibility criteria.

body.question: What is the major compliance after conversion?
body.answer: The company must update all legal records, PAN, TAN, GST and inform banks and authorities.

body.question: Will the company’s name change after conversion?
body.answer: Yes, the suffix will change from “Private Limited” to “Limited”.

body.question: Can the converted company list on the stock exchange immediately?
body.answer: No. Separate listing procedures with SEBI must be followed.

body.question: What if I fail to file MGT-14 within 30 days?
body.answer: A penalty will be levied and condonation of delay must be sought.

body.question: Can Professionals Law assist with post-conversion compliance?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law provides full support in post-conversion filings and statutory updates.

body.question: Is physical presence required for the conversion process?
body.answer: No, the entire process can be handled online with Professionals Law’s remote services.
